ogTitle: Karawatha vs Karragarra Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Karawatha and Karragarra Island.
heading: Karawatha vs Karragarra Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Karawatha, Queensland and Karragarra Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Karawatha has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Karragarra Island (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Karawatha or Karragarra Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Karawatha has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Karragarra Island scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Karawatha scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Karragarra Island scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Karawatha or Karragarra Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Karawatha has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
